faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: organic, mutated creature designs created by retrovirus
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: diverse hostile lifeforms representing 'new and deadly forms of life' unleashed by biowar
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: creatures inhabit Avaron surface; manifest throughout 8-stage level progression representing different planetary regions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: hostile planetary environment with organic obstacles, mutated creature designs, and hazardous terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: high-speed enemy movement and dense attack patterns reflecting aggressive biological adaptation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: small player ship against overwhelming environmental and creature density; limited lives system
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must scout and retreat (mission: ascertain conditions and return to Odysseus)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: eight distinct level environments representing different Avaron regions, each with escalating danger indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: progression through levels demonstrates increasing hostile conditions; final 3 levels locked behind difficulty threshold

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: colorful, deep bass-heavy aesthetic with creative artwork emphasizing alien/organic design language
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: creatures attack relentlessly; environment itself becomes weapon through crushing mechanics and hazard placement

environmental_storytelling.example: obstacles in scenery and landscape create visual narrative of complex, layered hostile terrain without blocking player collision
diverse mutated lifeforms across 8 levels visually communicate different ecological zones and retrovirus adaptation patterns
progression from least-hostile to increasingly hostile designated zones; final 3 levels unavailable at lower difficulties signal extreme danger
deep bass-heavy music paired with colorful, creative artwork emphasizes alien biosphere identity and survival stakes
fast scrolling speed combined with increasing foreground/background obstacle complexity communicates planetary conditions deteriorating

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: 8-directional movement with independent shot trajectory; can place Power Star to block projectiles
destroyed and respawns with one less life; visual reset indicates survival system in operation
extremely fast movement and projectile fire; dense attack patterns increase with level progression
lethal crushing force when player ship trapped between screen edge and wall; otherwise non-lethal collision
placed by player to intercept enemy projectiles; demonstrates active threat mitigation strategy

notes: Bio-Hazard Battle communicates world-state primarily through visual density, creature diversity, and environmental complexity rather than explicit UI. The game's core manifestation layer emphasizes biological contamination through organic enemy design and hazardous terrain. Mission narrative (scout Avaron, assess habitability, return to Odysseus) is supported by escalating difficulty and level progression. Four distinct player ships with varying flight speeds and weapons create faction identity differentiation. The fast scrolling speed and deep musical composition reinforce environmental hostility and urgency of the biowar survival scenario.
